---
output: github_document
---

<p align="center">
  <img src="man/figures/logo.jpg" alt="AfriAtlas R Package Logo" width="180">
</p>

# AfriAtlas: Visualizing African Bird Atlas Data

The **`AfriAtlas`** R package provides robust, publication-ready tools for ecologists and enthusiasts to download, process, and visualize pentad-level bird atlas data from the **African Bird Atlas Project (ABAP)** and its associated country projects (like SABAP2).

The package is designed for portability and efficiency, handling dynamic boundaries via the `rnaturalearth` package and enforcing API safety limits.

---

## 🚀 Installation

You can install the development version of `AfriAtlas` directly from GitHub using the `remotes` package.

```{r install, eval = FALSE}
# Install necessary dependencies if you haven't already
if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("remotes")
}


# Install AfriAtlas 
remotes::install_github("bdanmallam/AfriAtlas")
Quick Usage Examples
Once installed, the package provides three core functions:

##1. Map Species Distribution (map_species)
Generates a map of species sightings versus coverage, including a widespreadness pie chart. Execution is halted if more than 10 species are requested.

```{r eval=FALSE}
library(AfriAtlas)

# Generate a single species map for South Africa (Pin-tailed Whydah)
map_species(species_codes = 846, country = "Nigeria")

# Generate maps for a batch of species in Kenya (saved as PNGs)
map_species(species_codes = c(129, 311), country = "Kenya")
```

## 2. Map Survey Effort (map_coverage)
Generates a map showing the total full-protocol survey hours (effort) per pentad. Categories are dynamically adjusted based on the maximum effort level found in the country.

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Generate effort map for Nigeria 
map_coverage("Nigeria")

# Generate effort map for South Africa 
map_coverage("South Africa")
```

## 3. Review and Access Codes (get_species_code)
Utility functions to access the internal species data before running mapping functions.

```{r eval = FALSE}
library(AfriAtlas)

# A. Explore the full dataset (opens in a viewer pane)
AfriAtlas::explore_species_codes() 

# B. Get a specific species code by common name
code <- AfriAtlas::get_species_code("Common Bulbul")
print(code)
```

## Further Documentation
For detailed guides, examples, and technical explanations, please refer to the package vignette:

```{r eval = FALSE}
vignette("AfriAtlas_tutorial")
```
